frames: The agent begins by pushing its lower body off the ground, extending its legs.
It continues to rise, bending its upper body and lifting its torso from a prone position.
The agent achieves an upright, hunched posture, balancing on its two lower leg segments.
It maintains this stable, upright stance, with its upper body slightly bent forward.
The agent exhibits minor swaying, adjusting its balance while remaining stationary.
Its posture remains largely unchanged, with its lower legs firmly on the ground.
The agent shows slight, controlled movements in its upper body, maintaining equilibrium.
It concludes the clip in a stable, upright, and slightly hunched position, without any forward locomotion.
